subject: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 30 May
deck_filename: Counter Taobao Weekly Update - Week ending 30 May.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Performance Dashboard (as of 28 May)

A/ Topline/P&L

* Local SIP May MTD seller PC2 unavailable due to BI data logic update; May
proj is best effort basis while ongoing validation of P&L data after BPSF
raw data update

** Combined PC2 excludes SLS P&L

Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] May proj ADO 214k, 127% RR, %CB +0.9pp MoM; ADG $2.4M, 121%RR,
   %CB +1.0pp MoM
   1.

      Local SIP ADO 131% RR, %CB +0.7pp MoM; high RR driven by 1) new SKU
      ads incubation utilising BPSF budget 2) upsized item rebates
contributed to
      both traffic and CR improvement
      2.

      CNSIP ADO 97% RR, %CB maintained MoM; underperformance due to SKUs
      frozen by CB ops on 5/11 due to payment binding issue; next step to deep
      dive binding failure reasons and improve overall binding rate
      2.

   [P&L] May proj combined PC2 $3.4M, $1.7M over-performance
   1.

      Local SIP 1.8M out-performance, mainly driven by 1)
      faster-than-expected local SIP seller fraud case resolution 2) topline
      out-performance leading to budget surplus 3) BPSF budget uplift
      2.

      CN SIP hitting target; MoM decline expected driven by 1) SIP ads
      investment -2.8pp MoM with SIP reducing CNSIP mark-up 2) import market
      platform PRM +1.2 p.p MoM


 B/ SIP fraud management update

   1.

   Fraud detection: 1 new fraud seller confirmed this week on IDSIP; across
   all lanes MTD total 331 fraud sellers, 83.1k fraud orders, $2.2M total
   loss, 28% recovered from clawback
   2.

   IDSIP COD re-opening: 95% COD ADO coverage reopened; BAU weekly process
   to identify new batch of “safe” sellers to reopen
   3.

   COD rate and cod fail rate are analyzed in separate email: IDSIP fraud
   update - 27 May
   4.

   Long-term product solution to a) settle sellers when destination order
   arrive at end status (complete, cancel, rts etc) b) fraud checker for ops
   daily handling: go-live ETA 10

*Abandonment rate: *Overall 115% RR; ID 80% RR due to high chats

*Avg Waiting time: *Overall missing target with 23% RR
ID 13% RR, due to a large volume of incoming chats diverted from
SG 58% RR; avg waiting time increased WoW mainly due to the
MY 45% RR; due to spike in chats diverted from chatbot A/B testing
*SIP CS relocation to PH: *pending CB SCS pilot results (PH and BR
#2 Added to RBX P0 feature list. Pending Price PM availability to
#3 Live test sign-off by 30/May
#4 Local FBS x SIP enhancement UAT (Order) ongoing, UAT (supply
#16 RBX approved, to involve RSX evaluation due to effort estimation
#17 UAT timeline 2/Jun-4/Jun, Live testing timeline 10/Jun-12/Jun,
#18 HOD approval for P1 received. Raised to May roadmap for RBX
Direct Performance: MTD ADO 7.4k (107% RR); SKU count 104% RR; SKU
# sellers behind target mainly due to slow seller action after
Short-term: policy tightening + RM/SCA reminder + Biz ops to offer
Long-term: product solution to bypass seller steps in onboarding;
weekly acquisition tracking 3) internal system/process training to RM

content.answer:
Hi Ian,


*Slide 3*

   -

   Do we have a week by week target at least for the next couple of months
   for both ERPs? # of sellers, SKUs, ADO etc?

We’re aligning ERP target with Jason team and CN team,  target to share
monthly target in next monthly slide

*Slide 10*

   -

   Are we at a stage right now that we should be rejecting sellers? We
   should just take all sellers?
   -

   What is the reason that sellers will reject us right now? And what
   exactly are they rejecting? I thought they agreed to onboard to begin with?

For acquired sellers that accept to join Shopee, we will not reject onboard
these sellers. Sellers can open their shops without incentives. However,
since resources are offered to the sellers, market prefer to focus
resources on sellers that matches market demand, hence we allow for market
level selection to join program

Main rejection reasons are (1) sellers not having sufficient manpower (2)
their focus is in selective/other markets. We will re-pitch these sellers
using ERP solution again

*Slide 14*

   -

   So bad for SG/MY? We should not be too affected by actions of 3PF if the
   growth lever should be via sellers on SLS? Feels very odd that both markets
   are just not growing at all

To clarify on how 3PF fee impacts SLS sellers

   -

   Current MY ADO contribution: 57% from 3PF, 38% from CNLS, 4% from SLS
   -

   Most 3PF sellers use SLS channel, either as backup fulfillment or they
   are “fake 3PF” - this group is impacted by 3PF fee increase despite
   fulfilling via SLS

We deep-dived to SG/MY ADO drop

   -

   [-77 ADO] 3PF fee increase leading to seller lower ads / higher pricing;
   Jun focus on pushing seller to recover investment, and work with SG/MY to
   reinvest PC2; ETA 13 Jun
   -

   [-60 ADO] Top sellers incur penalty points blocking them from campaign
   participation; in progress of identifying reasons for penalty
   (system/program/seller issu

content.question:
We probably need to start seeing some ADO impact from these initiatives..



*From:* Peiya Xia <peiya.xia@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Saturday, 7 June 2025 11:47 am
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>; Jianghong Liu <
jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin
Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <
yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <
wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Momo Chen <momo.chen@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <
wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>;
Steven Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>; Wei Loong Yong <
weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Nicholas Lim <nicholas.lim@shopee.com>; Keith
Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>; Joe Li <joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Ren Jiahui <
jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Lee Jia Yi <jiayi.jylee@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <
eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 30 May



Hi Ian,



*Slide 3*

· Do we have a week by week target at least for the next couple of months
for both ERPs?
# of sellers, SKUs, ADO etc?

We’re aligning ERP target with Jason team and CN team,  target to share
monthly target in next monthly slide

*Slide 10*

· Are we at a stage right now that we should be rejecting sellers?

We
should just take all sellers?
· What is the reason that sellers will reject us right now?

And what
exactly are they rejecting?
I thought they agreed to onboard to begin with?

For acquired sellers that accept to join Shopee, we will not reject onboard
these sellers. Sellers can open their shops without incentives. However,
since resources are offered to the sellers, market prefer to focus
resources on sellers that matches market demand, hence we allow for market
level selection to join program

Main rejection reasons are (1) sellers not having sufficient manpower (2)
their focus is in selective/other markets. We will re-pitch these sellers
using ERP solution again

*Slide 14*

· So bad for SG/MY?

We should not be too affected by actions of 3PF if the
growth lever should be via sellers on SLS?

Feels very odd that both markets
are just not growing at all

To clarify on how 3PF fee impacts SLS sellers

· Current MY ADO contribution: 57% from 3PF, 38% from CNLS, 4% from SLS

· Most 3PF sellers use SLS channel, either as backup fulfillment or they
are “fake 3PF” - this group is impacted by 3PF fee increase despite
fulfilling via SLS

We deep-dived to SG/MY ADO drop

· [-77 ADO] 3PF fee increase leading to seller lower ads / higher pricing;
Jun focus on pushing seller to recover investment, and work with SG/MY to
reinvest PC2; ETA 13 Jun

· [-60 ADO] Top sellers incur penalty points blocking them from campaign
participation; in progress of identifying reasons for penalty
(system/program/seller issue); then act accordingly

· [-57 ADO] Top sellers moving to CNLS; next step to include CNLS ADO into
tracking; ETA 13 Jun



*Slide 15*

· For #2, have they started selling in markets?
· For #4, is this sourcing translating to orders?
